fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Hawaii Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Hawaii - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity hawaii, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to hawaii, respiratory therapist compact hawaii, hawaii respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Hawaii PVL — Respiratory Therapy
board.parent_dept: Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Care Assurance
board.mailing_address: Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs, Professional and Vocational Licensing Division, P. O. Box 3469, Honolulu, Hawaii 96801

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Hawaii.

steps.title: Create MyPVL Account and Start Application
steps.description: Navigate to the MyPVL portal (https://pvl.ehawaii.gov/mypvl/), create an account, log in, and select 'Apply for a License' for Respiratory Therapist.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form within MyPVL and submit the non-refundable application and initial license fees.

steps.title: Arrange for NBRC Verification
steps.description: Request the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) to directly send verification of your RRT credential to the Hawaii Board of Medical Examiners.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Record Check (CHRC)
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board (available on the Forms page) to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal history record check.

steps.title: Submit All Supporting Documents
steps.description: Ensure all other required documents, such as official transcripts and any other forms specified by the Board, are submitted either via MyPVL or mail.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Board of Medical Examiners will review your complete application and supporting documents. Upon approval, your license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Hawaii respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Hawaii have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Hawaii?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Hawaii
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Hawaii, navigate to the MyPVL online portal to create an account and begin your application.

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Navigate to the MyPVL portal at pvl.ehawaii.gov/mypvl/. 2. Click 'Create an Account' and follow the prompts to register with a valid email address and create a password. 3. Once your account is created and verified, log in to access the application dashboard. 4. Select 'Apply for a License' and choose 'Respiratory Therapist' from the available professions.
